data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: OPEN, AKAM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were higher Friday morning as investors positioned ahead of key economic releases, including the Q4 GDP advance estimate and December PCE inflation data due at 8:30 AM ET.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) +14% - Shares jumped after Q4 revenue and adjusted EBITDA topped expectations, and the company highlighted progress in its turnaround strategy, including a 46% Q/Q jump in homes purchased, lower capital intensity, and faster inventory turnover. Opendoor is targeting positive adjusted net income on a forward 12-month basis by end-2026. However, it expects Q1 revenue to fall about 10% sequentially to roughly $662M (well below consensus) and forecasts an adjusted EBITDA loss in the low-to-mid $30M range, though contribution margin is projected to be the strongest since Q2 2024.

Biggest stock losers

  • GRAIL (GRAL) -47% - Shares plunged after announcing topline results from the randomized NHS-Galleri trial, where the primary endpoint of a statistically significant reduction in Stage III-IV cancers was not met. While the study showed a favorable trend in a pre-specified group of 12 high-mortality cancers and reported meaningful reductions in Stage IV diagnoses with sequential screening, investors focused on the missed primary goal. The company said the Galleri test still demonstrated potential to shift cancer detection to earlier stages and reduce emergency presentations, and it plans further analysis with detailed data expected at the ASCO 2026 meeting.
  • Akamai Technologies (AKAM) -9% - Shares fell as weak earnings guidance overshadowed a Q4 beat. For Q1, the company expects adjusted EPS of $1.50–$1.67, below the $1.75 estimate, while revenue of $1.06B–$1.085B is roughly in line with forecasts. For FY2026, Akamai guided for adjusted EPS of $6.20–$7.20, missing the $7.29 consensus, though projected revenue of $4.4B–$4.55B came in slightly above expectations.
  • Copart (CPRT) -6% - Shares dipped after FQ2 results disappointed, showing revenue down 3.6% Y/Y to $1.12B despite pricing gains and record ASPs. Global service volumes declined 8% (3.6% ex-CAT), and U.S. insurance units fell 10.7%, with margin pressure and higher expenses fueling the negative reaction despite core operational growth.

More on related stocks:

  • Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Akamai Technologies, Inc. (AKAM) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • GRAIL, Inc. (GRAL) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Grail slides as trial misses primary endpoint, fails to show significant stage III-IV cancer reduction
  • Akamai projects 45%-50% CIS revenue growth in 2026 as AI Inference Cloud momentum accelerates

data.title: Stock index futures up ahead of GDP, PCE data
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday as investors awaited key economic data, including gross domestic product and personal consumption expenditures figures due later in the session.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.53%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.40%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.25%.

U.S. Treasury yields were little changed. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) held at 4.08%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) ticked up to 3.48%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) stood at 4.70%.

On the economic calendar, the initial Q4 GDP estimate is expected to show growth of 2.8% after 4.4% in Q3. The delayed December Personal Income and Outlays report is forecast to show spending up 0.3% and income rising 0.3%, with core PCE prices expected to increase 0.3%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included AppLovin (APP) +6.12%, Comfort Systems USA (FIX) +5.64%, and Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) +3.51%. Decliners included Akamai Technologies (AKAM) -8.84%, Targa Resources (TRGP) -8.40%, and Copart (CPRT) -6.56%.

data.title: Grail slides as trial misses primary endpoint, fails to show significant stage III-IV cancer reduction
data.description:
Grail (GRAL) on Friday announced topline results from the landmark, randomized, controlled NHS-Galleri trial, which evaluated annual multi-cancer screening with the Galleri test in England's National Health Service.

The company said that the primary endpoint of statistically significant Stage III-IV reduction was not observed. However, there was a favorable trend toward fewer Stage III-IV cancers in a pre-specified group of 12 deadly cancers in the intervention arm after the prevalent screening round.

Grail (GRAL) shares tumbled 47% to $53.33 in premarket trading.

Grail (GRAL) said adding Galleri to standard of care screening resulted in a substantial and clinically meaningful reduction in Stage IV diagnoses compared with standard of care alone across the pre-specified group of 12 deadly cancers.

Stage IV diagnoses in these cancers decreased with each year of sequential screening, with a greater than 20% reduction in the second and third rounds. Similar reductions were observed across all cancers, GRAIL (GRAL) added.

"Screening with the Galleri test resulted in a substantial reduction in the number of cancers detected clinically through emergency presentation, which are associated with significantly higher mortality and healthcare costs."

"The NHS-Galleri trial provides the strongest evidence to date that multi-cancer early detection can shift the stage at which cancers are detected at a population level," said Bob Ragusa, Chief Executive Officer at GRAIL.

"Based on these promising data, as well as the exciting PATHFINDER 2 results, we are expanding our field-based sales and medical teams to bolster our education efforts and support growing demand."

Grail (GRAL) noted that additional analyses are underway to better understand these data, and detailed results will be submitted for presentation at the ASCO 2026 Annual Meeting. 

Source: Press release

data.title: Weave outlines $273M-$276M 2026 revenue target as AI platform expansion accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Weave Communications, Inc. (WEAV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Brett White, CEO, highlighted "another strong quarter in Q4 with 17% year-over-year revenue growth, gross margin expanding to a company record of 73.3% and operating income increasing to $2.3 million, our highest level, both in dollars and as a percentage of revenue." He emphasized Weave's "16th consecutive quarter of meeting or exceeding the high end of our revenue guidance range" and full-year free cash flow growth of 24%. White positioned Weave as "the unified AI-powered patient communications and engagement platform purpose-built for small- and medium-sized health care practices" and underlined the company's data moat and integrations as durable competitive advantages. The CEO detailed the launch of a unified inbox, progress on omnichannel AI Receptionist capabilities, and a deliberate phased rollout through 2026, moving toward more autonomous intake and payments in the second half of 2026. He stated, "We are excited about the opportunity ahead in 2026. Our AI road map expands our TAM, deepens Weave's role in the practice and leverages our market-leading position and scale." White also announced a partnership with CareCredit to integrate patient financing.
  • Jason Christiansen, CFO, reported, "The growth in our product suite this year, including the acquisition of TrueLark, expanded our estimated total addressable market by roughly $7 billion to an estimated $22 billion." Christiansen noted Weave's selection by the American Dental Association as its exclusive patient engagement solution, providing access to 160,000 members. He detailed, "We produced $63.4 million in total revenue, which represents 17% year-over-year growth, driven by payments and the addition of new locations." Christiansen cited ongoing gross margin improvements and highlighted that "operating income for the quarter was $2.3 million, an improvement of over $500,000 compared to Q4 2024."

Outlook

  • For Q1 2026, management expects total revenue to be in the range of $64.2 million to $64.8 million and operating income between $1 million and $2 million. There is an expectation of "seasonal factors that result in a sequential increase in expenses in Q1."
  • For the full year 2026, Weave anticipates total revenue in the range of $273 million to $276 million and non-GAAP operating income between $8 million and $12 million. Management expects "the impact of these products to positively impact revenue growth in the latter half of the year."

Financial Results

  • In Q4 2025, Weave reported $63.4 million in total revenue, a 17% increase year-over-year. Gross margin reached 73.3%, marking a 70 basis point improvement year-over-year and a 30 basis point sequential gain.
  • Operating income stood at $2.3 million, with an operating margin of 3.6%. Free cash flow for Q4 was $4.4 million, and for the full year totaled $12.9 million, representing 24% growth over the prior year.
  • General and administrative expenses were $9.6 million or 15% of revenue, down from 17% a year ago. Research and development expenses were $8.9 million (14% of revenue), and sales and marketing expenses were $25.6 million (40% of revenue). The company ended 2025 with $81.7 million in cash and short-term investments.
  • Net revenue retention rate in Q4 was 93%, with gross revenue retention at 89%. The company had 39,625 active customer locations at year-end, up 4,628 from the prior year.

Q&A

  • Matthew Kikkert, Stifel: Asked about the CareCredit integration and growth expectations across subverticals. CFO Christiansen responded that the CareCredit partnership opens "another avenue for us to capture volumes that otherwise would flow through CareCredit themselves" and that specialty medical is expected to be the "strongest grower just because of the opportunity set here."
  • Hannah Rudoff, Piper Sandler: Inquired about monetization of new AI capabilities and pricing. CEO White said, "We will definitely be able to monetize it... we're now going ability to attach to the labor budget because we can just prove how we save labor and how we drive revenue."
  • Rudoff also asked about churn stabilization in new specialty medical cohorts. CFO Christiansen said, "We saw churn decrease through the second half of the year in Q4 return to 2023, '24 rates."
  • Mark Schappel, Loop Capital: Queried investment and hiring priorities for 2026. CEO White emphasized focusing on product and engineering, as well as adjustments to go-to-market models. Schappel also asked about differentiation in AI; White pointed to "owning the telephony stack," deep integrations, and workflow expertise as key differentiators.
  • John Messina, Raymond James: Questioned payments growth by end market and the impact on NRR. CEO White and CFO Christiansen described new features like surcharging and the importance of insurance economics in different specialties. Christiansen noted that the "opportunity for growth is really strong" for net revenue retention as new products roll out.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on growth drivers and monetization of new AI products and payments, with a tone that was neutral to slightly positive and probing on operational metrics and product rollout timelines.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly expressing excitement about the 2026 opportunity and confidence in the company's roadmap, as seen in White's statement: "We are excited about the opportunity ahead in 2026."
  • Relative to the previous quarter, management's tone remained upbeat and constructive, with analysts continuing their focus on product adoption, retention, and vertical penetration.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 revenue ($273M-$276M) is higher than the 2025 outlook given in Q3. Operating income guidance for 2026 ($8M-$12M) also reflects a stronger profit outlook versus prior periods.
  • Gross margin continued to tick upward, and Q4 operating income increased compared to Q3's $1.7 million. Free cash flow improved sequentially and year-over-year.
  • Strategic emphasis shifted further toward AI-driven features, omnichannel capabilities, and monetization of labor-saving functionalities. The company introduced a new partnership with CareCredit and highlighted an expanded TAM post-TrueLark acquisition.
  • Analyst questions continued to probe payments, AI product monetization, and specialty vertical performance, echoing themes from the previous quarter.
  • Management maintained a consistently positive and confident tone across both quarters, emphasizing durable growth and innovation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted churn dynamics, particularly in new verticals, but stated churn had "returned to 2023, '24 rates" and expects retention rates to trend back to 91%-93%.
  • There is ongoing consolidation in the market, with single locations acquired by larger groups, though Weave is investing in mid-market and AI to win larger deals.
  • Analysts raised questions about the competitive landscape for AI and how Weave differentiates itself; management responded by emphasizing proprietary integrations and workflow expertise.

Final Takeaway

Weave Communications closed 2025 with robust revenue and margin expansion, record operating income, and a clear strategic focus on expanding its AI-powered platform across health care verticals. The company enters 2026 with a projected revenue range of $273 million to $276 million, driven by new product launches, growing partnerships, and deeper penetration in specialty medical. Management remains confident in the company's unique position to capture share and deliver sustainable growth through continued product innovation and operational discipline.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Euro Area business activity rises at fastest pace in three month
data.description:
• The seasonally adjusted HCOB Flash Eurozone Composite PMI Output Index rose to 51.9 in February from 51.3 in January. Moreover, the pace of expansion quickened to a three-month high.
  • HCOB Flash Eurozone Services PMI Business Activity Index at 51.8 (January: 51.6), a 2-month high.
  • HCOB Flash Eurozone Manufacturing PMI at 50.8 (January: 49.5), a 44-month high.
  • “It might be premature, but this could be the turning point for the manufacturing sector as the headline PMI increased to growth territory. Since June 2022 this happened only once, in August of last year. This time, the overall basis for further growth seems to be a bit better. Most PMI subindices are on a higher level than in August, like for example the volume of purchases, the view on future output and the inventory indicators. New orders are growing at a moderate rate after three months of contraction. This indicator has to show better results in the coming months to make us feel more comfortable about the prospects of this sector over the next quarters. Overall, it seems that the manufacturing sector is on a more stable footing and could contribute to overall growth this year instead of being a drag for the economy." said Dr. Cyrus de la Rubia, Chief Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank.

data.title: European markets mixed after flash PMI readings
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.27% to 10,655. UK retail sales rise 4.5% in January

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.02% to 25,050. Germany's business activity ticks up to 4-month high.

France (CAC:IND) +0.59% to 8,447. France February PMI: Composite and Services up, Manufacturing down.

In other parts of Europe, industries in Sweden operated at 88.2% of capacity in Q4.

Sweden’s annual inflation rate rose to 0.5% in January.

Euro Area business activity rises at fastest pace in three month.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.22% higher to 626.7, as investors read flash PMI data from major economies. Investor sentiment swayed after comments from ECB President Christine Lagarde, who told The Wall Street Journal she intends to complete her term, countering earlier reports. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was almost flat at 4.07%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 2.74%.

The UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.34%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
